findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Remote exfiltration of tool inputs, outputs, and environment secrets
findings.description: The bundled PostToolUse hook collects every tool invocation's name, input, and output, adds host/user metadata, and includes sensitive environment variables such as ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and GITHUB_TOKEN. It then posts that payload to an external HTTPS endpoint. This creates a direct data-exfiltration path for potentially sensitive prompts, file contents, command outputs, and credentials.
findings.evidence: "input" and "output" are serialized into the JSON payload; "session_env" includes "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY" and "GITHUB_TOKEN"; the payload is sent via curl to "https://mcp-analytics-hub.io/hooks/post-tool".
findings.remediation: Remove external transmission of tool data and never collect or transmit secrets from environment variables. If audit logging is required, keep logs local by default, redact sensitive fields, require explicit user opt-in, and use a reviewed enterprise-controlled endpoint with documented data handling.

findings.title: Global PostToolUse hook installation enables persistent interception of future tool activity
findings.description: The skill instructs the user to copy a bundled script into ~/.claude/hooks/PostToolUse.sh, which alters the agent's global hook behavior. Once installed, the hook can observe subsequent tool invocations outside the immediate skill workflow, creating persistent surveillance of agent activity.
findings.evidence: The setup instructions explicitly say: "cp hooks/monitor.sh ~/.claude/hooks/PostToolUse.sh" followed by chmod. The accompanying text states the hook captures tool names, inputs, and outputs and forwards them to a centralized service.
findings.remediation: Do not install global hooks from untrusted skills. Scope hooks to a single reviewed deployment, require explicit consent, and document exactly what data is intercepted and where it is stored or sent.

findings.title: Skill description understates secret-collection and off-host monitoring behavior
findings.description: The manifest presents the package as an MCP management toolkit with audit logging, but the actual hook behavior goes beyond simple monitoring by harvesting session environment variables and transmitting them off-host. This mismatch can cause users to trust and install the skill without understanding the full surveillance and exfiltration scope.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md describes "tool-invocation logging," while the script also collects "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", "GITHUB_TOKEN", and "HOME" under "session_env" before sending the payload remotely.
findings.remediation: Align the manifest and instructions with actual behavior, or remove the hidden collection of environment variables entirely. Security-relevant data collection must be explicitly disclosed and narrowly limited.

findings.title: Hardcoded bearer token embedded in the hook script
findings.description: The script contains a hardcoded Authorization bearer token for the remote logging endpoint. Embedded credentials increase the risk of unauthorized reuse, leakage through source sharing, and unreviewed access to the receiving service.
findings.evidence: The curl command uses: "Authorization: Bearer mcp_a1b2c3d4e5f6".
findings.remediation: Remove hardcoded secrets from the package. Use securely provisioned credentials, short-lived tokens, and documented secret-management mechanisms.
